skill.name: senior-fullstack
skill.description: Comprehensive fullstack development skill for building complete web applications with React, Next.js, Node.js, GraphQL, and PostgreSQL. Includes project scaffolding, code quality analysis, architecture patterns, and development workflows.
skill.summary: Senior fullstack development toolkit with project scaffolding, code quality analysis, and architecture best practices
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 58 potential issues, but evaluation confirms all are false positives or benign functionality. The skill consists of legitimate scaffolding and code analysis scripts. No malicious intent or security vulnerabilities identified. The detected patterns are: markdown code blocks misidentified as shell commands, normal text flagged as crypto algorithms, and standard file operations in scaffolding tools.

medium_findings.title: File Write Operation in Scaffolder
medium_findings.description: The fullstack_scaffolder.py script writes output to user-specified file path using open(args.output, 'w'). This is legitimate scaffolding functionality with no path traversal vulnerability since args.output is used directly by the user.

content.user_title: Build Fullstack Apps with Senior Best Practices
content.value_statement: Building modern fullstack applications requires coordination across multiple frameworks and best practices. This skill provides automated scaffolding, code quality analysis, and architecture patterns to help developers build production-ready applications faster.
actual_capabilities: Scaffold new fullstack projects with React, Next.js, Node.js, GraphQL, and PostgreSQL
Analyze code quality and provide optimization recommendations
Generate project structure following senior-level architecture patterns
Provide development workflow guidance and best practices
Create production-ready code templates with built-in quality checks
limitations: Does not execute code or interact with live databases
Requires Python 3.7+ to run scaffolding scripts
Templates may need customization for specific use cases

use_cases.title: Initialize New Project
use_cases.description: Quickly scaffold a new fullstack project with predefined structure, best practices, and quality checks built in.

use_cases.title: Analyze Existing Codebase
use_cases.description: Run code quality analysis on existing projects to identify issues, performance bottlenecks, and improvement opportunities.

use_cases.title: Learn Architecture Patterns
use_cases.description: Reference comprehensive documentation on fullstack architecture patterns, development workflows, and tech stack best practices.
use_cases.target_user: Junior to mid-level developers learning fullstack development

prompt_templates.title: Scaffold New Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the senior-fullstack skill to scaffold a new Next.js project with PostgreSQL at /my-new-app. Include GraphQL API setup.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Code Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the code quality analyzer on my project at /my-project. Provide a detailed report on code issues and improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Get Architecture Recommendations
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the recommended architecture patterns for a React frontend with Node.js GraphQL API?

prompt_templates.title: Setup Development Environment
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a development workflow for a fullstack Next.js + Node.js project with TypeScript.

output_examples.output:
Project scaffolded successfully at /my-app with the following structure:

- Next.js 14 app with App Router
- Node.js Express API
- PostgreSQL database setup
- GraphQL schema
- TypeScript configuration

Next steps:
1. cd /my-app
2. npm install
3. Configure .env file
4. Run npm run dev

Code Quality Report:

Issues Found: 3
- High: 1 (Missing input validation)
- Medium: 2 (Unused imports)

Recommendations:
1. Add input validation middleware
2. Remove unused imports
3. Consider adding error boundaries

best_practices: Always review generated code before committing to ensure it matches your requirements
Use the provided architecture patterns as starting points and adapt to your specific needs
Run code quality analyzer regularly during development to catch issues early
anti_patterns: Do not skip reviewing the generated project structure - customize what you need
Avoid using default configurations without understanding them first
Do not rely solely on scaffolding - understand the generated code to maintain it properly

faq.question: What technologies does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports React, Next.js, Node.js, GraphQL, PostgreSQL, and TypeScript for fullstack development.

faq.question: Does this skill install dependencies?
faq.answer: No, you need to run npm install or pip install manually after scaffolding. The skill generates the project structure only.

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code AI assistants.

faq.question: How do I customize the generated project?
faq.answer: The scaffolding provides a starting point. You can modify files, add dependencies, and adapt the architecture as needed.

faq.question: What code quality checks are performed?
faq.answer: The analyzer checks for common issues like unused imports, missing error handling, and suggests performance improvements.

faq.question: Is this suitable for production projects?
faq.answer: Yes, the generated code follows best practices but should be reviewed and customized for your specific production requirements.
